source_evidence.supplemental_operations_note: The documented GET /users/me identity is supplemental because it is omitted from the pinned OpenAPI paths map; recording it preserves the reviewed 250-operation corpus and is not an executable capability grant.

scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every endpoint in Asana's official OpenAPI spec (250 operations, including /users/me which the spec omits from its paths map but which is a real, documented, already-used identity alias) is enumerated below, each covered_by a stream/write or excluded with a specific reason. 12 streams covering 13 GET rows (4 pre-existing + 9 new rows) plus 13 write actions (all new; legacy shipped none) cover the core PM domain (workspaces/projects/tasks/sections/tags/stories/users/teams/custom_fields/project_statuses/memberships); the remaining ~224 endpoints are duplicate/filtered views of already-covered data, admin/Enterprise-gated configuration, or entire adjacent product sub-domains (Goals, Portfolios, Time Tracking, Webhooks, Project/Task Templates, Allocations/Budgets/Rates) scoped out as future capability-expansion passes of their own rather than partially folded into this one — see docs.md's Known limits for the full breakdown.

excluded.reason: uploads a file (multipart/form-data or an external URL reference) as a task/project attachment — not a JSON record body the write dialect's body_type json/form/none shapes can express
single-custom-field detail lookup, already covered in full by the custom_fields stream's getAll-shaped list

custom_field_settings is the join/association between a custom_fields definition (already covered by the custom_fields stream) and a parent object (goal/portfolio/project/team) — the association itself carries no data beyond which custom field is attached to which parent, redundant with reading custom_fields directly plus the parent object's own custom_field_settings summary field

the generic Memberships resource is a newer unified access-control API covering the same access-grant data already exposed per-object by the covered team_memberships/workspace_memberships streams and by portfolio/project membership endpoints (also excluded below as duplicates) — redundant surface, not independently new data

the generic Memberships resource is a newer unified access-control API covering the same access-grant data already exposed per-object by the covered team_memberships/workspace_memberships streams and by portfolio/project membership endpoints (also excluded below as duplicates) — redundant surface, not independently new data

custom_field_settings is the join/association between a custom_fields definition (already covered by the custom_fields stream) and a parent object (goal/portfolio/project/team) — the association itself carries no data beyond which custom field is attached to which parent, redundant with reading custom_fields directly plus the parent object's own custom_field_settings summary field

project membership records — the same generic-Memberships-vs-per-object-membership redundancy as team_memberships/workspace_memberships' relationship to the unified Memberships resource, here scoped to projects specifically; not independently modeled as its own stream in this pass

single-status detail/delete for a record the project_statuses stream already reads in full; not implemented as a separate write action in this pass (see docs.md — project status posts are typically append-only progress updates, better matched to a future create_project_status write than a delete-focused one)

single-status detail/delete for a record the project_statuses stream already reads in full; not implemented as a separate write action in this pass (see docs.md — project status posts are typically append-only progress updates, better matched to a future create_project_status write than a delete-focused one)
single-project detail lookup, already covered in full by the projects stream's getAll-shaped list
attaches/detaches a custom_field_settings association to a project — same association-record redundancy as the custom_field_settings read endpoints above

custom_field_settings is the join/association between a custom_fields definition (already covered by the custom_fields stream) and a parent object (goal/portfolio/project/team) — the association itself carries no data beyond which custom field is attached to which parent, redundant with reading custom_fields directly plus the parent object's own custom_field_settings summary field

project membership records — the same generic-Memberships-vs-per-object-membership redundancy as team_memberships/workspace_memberships' relationship to the unified Memberships resource, here scoped to projects specifically; not independently modeled as its own stream in this pass
attaches/detaches a custom_field_settings association to a project — same association-record redundancy as the custom_field_settings read endpoints above
reorders sections within a project's board view — a display-ordering action, not new data, narrower than create/update_section already covered
aggregate task-count-by-status for a project, derivable by counting the already-covered tasks stream client-side; no data beyond what tasks itself exposes
project-scoped task list, a filtered view of the same tasks stream already reads in full via its own optional project query param
single-section detail lookup, already covered in full by the sections stream's per-project getAll-shaped list
moves an existing task into a section — equivalent to setting update_task's memberships/section field; narrower than the already-covered update_task action
section-scoped task list, a filtered view of the same tasks stream already reads in full via its own project/section-capable query surface

the generic Status Updates resource (narrative progress posts on ANY object: project/portfolio/goal) overlaps the already-covered project_statuses stream's project-scoped equivalent; not modeled as a second, broader stream in this pass

the generic Status Updates resource (narrative progress posts on ANY object: project/portfolio/goal) overlaps the already-covered project_statuses stream's project-scoped equivalent; not modeled as a second, broader stream in this pass

single-story detail lookup, already covered in full by the stories stream's per-task getAll-shaped list
single-tag detail lookup, already covered in full by the tags stream's getAll-shaped list
tag-scoped task list, a filtered view of the same tasks stream already reads in full
single-task detail lookup, already covered in full by the tasks stream's getAll-shaped list
adds a task to an additional project — equivalent to update_task's projects field; narrower than the already-covered update_task action
adds one tag to a task — equivalent to update_task's tags field; narrower than the already-covered update_task action
reverse lookup of which projects a task belongs to — the same relationship data the tasks stream's own membership fields (via opt_fields) already expose; not modeled as an independent stream
removes a task from a project — same reasoning as addProject
removes one tag from a task — same reasoning as addTag
re-parents an existing task — equivalent to update_task's parent field, already covered
subtask list, a filtered view of the same tasks stream already reads in full (subtasks share the tasks schema; the tasks stream's opt_fields can surface a task's parent field)
creating a subtask is create_task with its parent field set — already expressible via the covered create_task action, not a separate action
task-scoped tag list, a filtered view of the same tags stream already reads in full (and the tasks stream's own opt_fields can already surface a task's tags)
single-team-membership detail lookup, already covered in full by the team_memberships stream's getAll-shaped list
single-team detail lookup, already covered in full by the teams stream's getAll-shaped list

custom_field_settings is the join/association between a custom_fields definition (already covered by the custom_fields stream) and a parent object (goal/portfolio/project/team) — the association itself carries no data beyond which custom field is attached to which parent, redundant with reading custom_fields directly plus the parent object's own custom_field_settings summary field
team-scoped project list, a filtered view of the same projects stream already reads in full (Asana's projects list endpoint itself accepts a team-scoping equivalent via workspace)
team-scoped project creation, a narrower variant of the already-covered create_project action (which accepts an optional team field in its record body)
team-scoped membership list, a filtered view of the same team_memberships stream already reads in full via its own optional team query param
team-scoped user list, a filtered view of the same users stream already reads in full via its own optional workspace query param
single-user detail lookup, already covered in full by the users stream's getAll-shaped list
user-scoped membership list, a filtered view of the same team_memberships data (Asana's own team query param on the covered stream can be combined with client-side filtering)
user-scoped team membership list, a filtered view of the same teams/team_memberships data already covered
user-scoped membership list, a filtered view of the same workspace_memberships data already covered
single-workspace-membership detail lookup, already covered in full by the workspace_memberships stream's getAll-shaped list
workspace-scoped project list, a filtered view of the same data the projects stream already reads in full via its own optional workspace query param
workspace-scoped project creation, a narrower variant of the already-covered create_project action (which accepts an optional workspace field in its record body)
keyword search over the same project records the projects stream already reads in full — a filtered/narrower view, not new data
workspace-scoped tag list, a filtered view of the same data the tags stream already reads in full via its own optional workspace query param
workspace-scoped tag creation, a narrower variant of the already-covered create_tag action (which accepts an optional workspace field in its record body)
single-task lookup by an organization's custom numeric id scheme, an alternate key for the same tasks stream data already covered
advanced keyword/field search over the same task records the tasks stream already reads in full — a filtered/narrower view, not new data
workspace-scoped user list, a filtered view of the same users stream already reads in full via its own optional workspace query param
single-user-in-workspace detail lookup, already covered in full by the users stream
meta-endpoint that executes up to 10 arbitrary sub-requests in one call; a request-multiplexing transport optimization, not itself a domain object or action
workspace-level custom OBJECT TYPE (not custom field) schema definitions — a schema-introspection endpoint for a newer Asana platform feature (custom item types), not task/project domain data
single custom-type schema lookup, same reasoning as the list

webhook-style change-event polling cursor feed (requires a sync_token from a prior call), not a listable domain-object stream; better modeled as CDC/webhooks, out of scope for a declarative full-refresh/incremental stream
async job-status poll for a previously-started long-running operation (e.g. duplicate/export); has no independent list endpoint and no meaning without the specific job gid a prior action returned
fiscal/reporting time-period definitions (quarters/fiscal years) used to scope Budgets/Allocations reporting — configuration metadata for the excluded financial add-on suite, not PM domain data
fiscal/reporting time-period definitions (quarters/fiscal years) used to scope Budgets/Allocations reporting — configuration metadata for the excluded financial add-on suite, not PM domain data
authenticated-user identity probe, used only by Check, not a syncable object stream
same change-event polling shape as /events, scoped to a workspace
autocomplete/search-as-you-type lookup tool for interactive UIs, not a syncable object list
Asana AI agent (bot actor) identity lookup — a narrow AI-feature-specific resource, not core PM domain data

Resource/Workload Management allocations (assigning a user's time-percentage to a project over a date range) is a paid-tier add-on planning feature layered on top of the already-covered projects/users streams, not core task/project sync data

requires a parent object gid (task/project/etc) plus attachment binary metadata; listing attachments per-task would need a fan_out over tasks with no independent value beyond what the tasks stream's own attachment_count-style fields already summarize, and every attachment record's useful payload is a download URL, not sync-relevant structured data
single-attachment detail lookup, same reasoning as the list endpoint
deleting an attachment is only meaningful paired with the excluded read/create attachment endpoints above

creating a custom field definition is a workspace-schema-design action (defining what data CAN be tracked), materially different in kind from populating task/project data records — narrower value than the task/project/section/tag CRUD already covered, and workspace-schema mutations carry outsized blast radius (affects every task using that field) for this pass
same workspace-schema-design scope narrowing as creating a custom field
same workspace-schema-design scope narrowing; additionally irreversibly destroys the field's data on every task that used it
adds a new picklist value to an enum-type custom field's definition — schema-design scope, same reasoning as create/updateCustomField
reorders an enum-type custom field's picklist values — same schema-design scope
renames/enables/disables one picklist value — same schema-design scope
triggers an async whole-workspace relationship-graph export job (returns a job gid to poll) — a bulk backup/migration tool, not an incremental sync-relevant action
triggers an async single-resource export job — same bulk-export-tool scope as exports/graph
